182.reason: Missing break statement before default; after processing '/', it falls through to default, causing incorrect token addition

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing break statement before default; after processing '/', it falls through to default, causing incorrect token addition
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing break statement before default case; after processing '/', it falls through to default and incorrectly adds '/' as a token again.

84.reason: Operator precedence: && binds tighter than ||, causing IndexOutOfBoundsException when last char is digit or line ends with identifier. Should be: while (pos < line.length() && (isIdent(line.charAt(pos)) || isDigit(line.charAt(pos))))

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Operator precedence: && binds tighter than ||, causing IndexOutOfBoundsException when last char is digit or line ends with identifier. Should be: while (pos < line.length() && (isIdent(line.charAt(pos)) || isDigit(line.charAt(pos))))

181.reason: Missing break statement; execution falls through to default case, causing the '/' to be processed again as a default token.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing break statement; execution falls through to default case, causing the '/' to be processed again as a default token.

19.reason: Operator precedence: && binds tighter than ||, causing potential index out-of-bounds when pos >= length and isDigit is evaluated; should be `pos < line.length() && (isIdent(line.charAt(pos)) || isDigit(line.charAt(pos)))`.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Operator precedence: && binds tighter than ||, causing potential index out-of-bounds when pos >= length and isDigit is evaluated; should be `pos < line.length() && (isIdent(line.charAt(pos)) || isDigit(line.charAt(pos)))`.

171.reason: Missing `break` after the case '/': block; fall-through to default case causes further processing of the same character, leading to potential errors.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing `break` after the case '/': block; fall-through to default case causes further processing of the same character, leading to potential errors.
